name: La Capitana Loop from Rincón de la Victoria
name_original: Ruta de los Tres Ríos (Benagalbón, Granadillas, Totalán) – circular desde Rincón de la Victoria
description: This moderate 17.2-mile mountain biking loop from Rincón de la Victoria offers 2058 feet of climbing and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Rincón De La Victoria offers diverse landscapes across the southern foothills of the Montes de Málaga. The region features an abrupt relief with elevations providing scenic climbs and descents. Trails wind through Mediterranean vegetation and offer rewarding sea views, making it suitable for various skill levels. The area benefits from a subtropical climate, supporting year-round outdoor activities. Best mountain bike trails around Rincón De La Victoria The most popular mountain bike trail is La Capitana Loop from Rincón de la Victoria, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Torre de Albenda – Junction to Camino del Tunante loop from Torre de Benagalbón, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.6 km) path. This route includes challenging climbs and descents with panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Paseo de la Marina – Coastal Section Near Málaga loop from Torre de Benagalbón, a 30.6 miles (49.2 km) trail leading through coastal sections, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Rincón De La Victoria is defined by its coastal features, Mediterranean vegetation, and the foothills of the Montes de Málaga. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Rincón De La Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Rincón De La Victoria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Rincón De La Victoria, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1200 times.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Rincón De La Victoria enjoys a subtropical climate with over 300 days of sunshine annually, making it suitable for mountain biking year-round. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, but even winter rides are often enjoyable.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Yes, Rincón De La Victoria offers trails suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32126615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Cantal – La Cala del Moral Beach loop from Benagalbón&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route, perfect for those new to mountain biking. The region has 11 easy routes in total.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: The terrain in Rincón De La Victoria is diverse, ranging from coastal sections to the foothills of the Montes de Málaga. You can expect trails winding through Mediterranean vegetation, offering scenic climbs and descents, and rewarding sea views. Some trails, like the 'Circuito del Guerre', feature technical sections with jumps and obstacles.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Rincón De La Victoria are circular. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994169582/la-capitana-loop-from-rincon-de-la-victoria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Capitana Loop from Rincón de la Victoria&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 27.7 km trail.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking?
A: While mountain biking, you can enjoy stunning sea views, especially along coastal sections and from higher elevations in the Montes de Málaga foothills. The Rincón de la Victoria Cliffs offer breathtaking vistas. You might also pass by highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Málaga Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; or viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/406395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del León Descent&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Rincón De La Victoria has 20 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17234214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de la Marina – Coastal Section Near Málaga loop from Torre de Benagalbón&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 49.2 km trail that includes significant elevation changes and technical sections.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: The mountain bike trails in Rincón De La Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of sea views and mountain terrain, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, beyond the trails, you can explore the Cueva del Tesoro (Treasure Cave), Europe's only accessible sea grotto, known for its unique formations and ancient cave paintings. The 18th-century Bezmiliana Castle, now an exhibition space, is another cultural highlight in the region.

Q: Where can I find parking or access trailheads for mountain biking in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Trailheads are generally accessible throughout the municipality. Specific parking information can vary by route, but Rincón De La Victoria is well-equipped for visitors. Many routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10301560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Albenda – Junction to Camino del Tunante loop from Torre de Benagalbón&lt;/a&gt;, start from easily reachable points within towns like Torre de Benagalbón.

Q: Are there options for bike rentals or guided tours in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Yes, services like bike rentals, including electric bikes and MTBs, and guided tours are available in Rincón De La Victoria. This makes it convenient for visitors to explore the extensive network of cycling routes even without bringing their own equipment.

Arroyo de Granadillas, a dry river bed from which one reaches La Capitana or Cerro Tío Cañas. Turn left or right and you can explore the area, it won't get boring. Or simply drive up and down the river bed, also very nice with the gravel bike.

A beautiful and flowery town in La Axarquía that lived through some bloody events at the beginning of the 20th century. The town rebelled against a possible electoral fraud that resulted in many injuries and arrests, as well as the death of a civil guard whose head rolled throughout the town and ended up in the river, never being found and turning the story into legend.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the La Capitana Loop mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While some sections might be accessible, the terrain includes significant elevation changes with strong climbs and rapid descents, particularly around the La Capitana area. It requires good fitness and technical skills, especially for mountain biking. Riders can expect a mix of dry riverbeds, tracks, and paths.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing the La Capitana Loop is approximately 2 hours and 35 minutes. This can vary depending on your fitness level, technical skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the La Capitana Loop?
faqs.answer: You will encounter varied terrain, including dry riverbeds (such as the Arroyo Granadillas), tracks ('carril'), and paths ('vereda'). The route features considerable elevation changes, with sustained climbs and descents. Some sections may be technical, offering challenges like drops, trialeras, jumps, and obstacles for mountain biking enthusiasts.

faqs.question: What are the best viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular panoramic views. From Cerro del Tío Cañas, you can see the Sierras Tejeda and Almijara, with Sierra Nevada often visible in the background. From Cerro Salazar (Capitán), the highest point, you'll get expansive views of the Totalán river valley, a large part of Málaga province, the bay, the Guadalhorce valley, and even the coast of North Africa. You'll also pass by the Torre de Albenda, a historical site, and the Benagalbón Village Center.

faqs.question: Is the La Capitana Loop from Rincón de la Victoria dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this route, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near settlements like Benagalbón Village Center. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and elevation changes, which might be strenuous for some dogs, and ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the La Capitana Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this loop is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for strenuous activity. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, making some sections slippery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Capitana Loop?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Rincón de la Victoria. You can typically find parking within the town itself, especially near the coastal areas or residential zones, and then cycle to the trailhead. Specific dedicated parking for this exact loop is not widely documented, so local street parking or public car parks in Rincón de la Victoria would be your best option.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the La Capitana Loop. It primarily uses public tracks and paths. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage if encountered.

faqs.question: Does the La Capitana Loop intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Rincón de la Victoria - Vélez-Málaga trail, the [CIMA ML10] El León * Totalán trail, Peñón del Cuervo - Olías, Arroyo Granadillas - La Capitana, Moclinejo - Totalán, Cerro Tío Caña - Cerro Benagalbón, and Arroyo Granadillas - Cerro Tío Caña.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and passes through the Benagalbón Village Center and begins in Rincón de la Victoria, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and purchase supplies before, during, or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is the La Capitana Loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating, significant elevation changes, and technical sections, the full La Capitana Loop is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginners. However, some shorter, less challenging sections or dedicated family-friendly singletracks might be found in the broader Rincón de la Victoria area, but not as part of this specific loop.
